articles.title: The NBA Play-In Is Here: Kalshi Gives Charlotte the Edge Over a Miami Team Built for This Moment
High Roller stock soars as much as 130% on Crypto.com prediction market agreement
Coinbase (COIN) and Robinhood (HOOD) best positioned in prediction market space, says Cantor
articles.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald analysts say Coinbase and Robinhood have the strongest positioning to capitalize on the growing prediction markets opportunity. The endorsement matters because both platforms have massive user bases and regulatory experience that could fast-track them past smaller competitors like Kalshi and Polymarket.

articles.title: Polymarket Reverses Course on Startups That Piggyback on Insider Trades
articles.summary: Polymarket has changed its policy on startups that build products around insider trading activity on the platform. The reversal signals growing regulatory pressure on prediction markets and could force traders to find new ways to exploit information asymmetries.

articles.title: A casino operator wants in on a $1T U.S. event trading market
articles.summary: A major casino operator is eyeing the massive U.S. event trading market, which analysts value at $1 trillion annually. This signals that traditional gambling companies see prediction markets as the next frontier for monetizing public sentiment on everything from elections to entertainment awards.

articles.title: Kalshi Rides Legal Winning Streak Into Montana
articles.summary: Kalshi is expanding into Montana after a series of legal victories that have strengthened its position in the prediction markets space. The move signals Kalshi's growing confidence in challenging state-level restrictions on political betting, potentially opening more markets for traders nationwide.

articles.title: Prediction Market Making Is Hard
articles.summary: Market makers are struggling to price prediction markets accurately, getting burned by information asymmetries and volatile swings that don't follow traditional financial patterns. This creates huge opportunities for informed bettors to exploit mispriced contracts, but also threatens the liquidity that makes these markets function in the first place.

articles.title: ‘Cowboy’: Michael Selig, a top Trump Wall Street regulator, comes out swinging
articles.summary: Michael Selig, a key Trump-era Wall Street regulator, is making aggressive moves that signal a major shift in financial oversight policy. His confrontational approach could reshape market regulations and create new volatility in financial sector stocks, making regulatory policy bets suddenly very interesting.

articles.title: Prediction markets have exploded. What can they tell us about Oregon’s hottest primary race? - Oregon Public Broadcasting
articles.summary: Oregon Public Broadcasting is covering how prediction markets are being used to handicap the state's most competitive primary election. It signals that mainstream media outlets are finally treating prediction markets as legitimate political forecasting tools worth covering alongside traditional polling.

articles.title: Kalshi Is Half Right About Prediction Markets and Gambling
articles.summary: Kalshi's defending prediction markets as fundamentally different from gambling, but they're only getting part of the argument right. The distinction matters because regulators are watching every move, and a weak defense could torpedo the entire industry's legitimacy.

articles.title: Iran war puts prediction markets back in the spotlight
articles.summary: Iran's escalating tensions with Israel and the broader Middle East conflict are driving massive volume on geopolitical prediction markets as traders scramble to price war scenarios. The surge proves that when real money is on the line, prediction markets often provide clearer intelligence than traditional polling or expert analysis about how conflicts will actually unfold.

articles.title: Robinhood excludes some prediction markets over manipulation fears
articles.summary: Robinhood is blocking access to certain prediction markets on its platform due to concerns about market manipulation. The move signals growing regulatory scrutiny that could force other platforms to restrict offerings and reshape which political and event markets remain accessible to retail bettors.
